sounds.summary: One take — one line. No overdubs, no corrections.
Four tracks. Field recordings, modulation, polyrhythmic abstraction. Stuttgart and surrounding areas.

bodyBlocks.text:
150011

Built from field recordings made in Stuttgart and surrounding areas. Recorded with contact microphones, XY stereo (Zoom H5), and wide stereo high-sensitivity mics.

The four tracks work through repetition, distortion, and layering. Polyrhythmic structures resist fixed grids. A filtered tone repeats as pulse. Percussion holds the frame. The rhythmic layer distorts within frequency constraints.

No edits, no second takes. One take — one line.

sounds.title: Resonanzraum Vita
sounds.summary: Site-specific recording and performance. House of Vita.

photos.alt: Live performance and recording at the house of Vita.
Live performance and recording at the house of Vita.

bodyBlocks.text:
Live performance and recording at the house of Vita.

The house has its own tone and quiet history. Light, walls, and furniture hold the memory of earlier rehearsals. We work with this memory as a resonant body. We listen to how air, wood, and instruments react to each other and let the space decide when the piece begins and ends.

sounds.title: learning to breathe
sounds.summary: Drones, pulses, and textured noise. Performed together in one take. No overdubs, no edits.

bodyBlocks.text: This album charts a meditative journey through states of being, using sound as both compass and vessel. With layers of drones, pulses, and textured noise, it explores the interplay between stillness and motion, chaos and harmony, the internal and the external.

It begins with Learning to Breathe, a grounding meditation where heartbeat rhythms and noise textures invite the listener to rediscover the breath as a unifying force. Stream of Consciousness then drifts into the restless ebb and flow of thought, gradually aligning scattered currents into a flowing whole.

From there, Particle Flow propels the journey into a realm of kinetic energy—micro- and macro-pulses colliding, carrying the listener into a state of perpetual movement. Taken together, these pieces form a continuum of sound—inviting deep listening, in the subtle rhythms that underlie both mind and matter.

sounds.title: Impact of the Fan
sounds.summary: Live studio session. Electromagnetic fields are modulated by airflow from a fan, shaping the sound in real time.

bodyBlocks.text: Live studio session. Electromagnetic fields are modulated by airflow from a fan, shaping the sound in real time.
